def: s.f. A noble woman, a lady, matron (a common termination of Mohammadan female names, e.g. Jamīla Ḵẖātūn)
subs: Queen of Paradise (a title given to Fātima, daughter of Mohammad).

def: s.f. A bedstead, couch, cot; a swing; a hammock
subs: (˚vā+an˚), s.m. A club shaped like the foot of a bedstead, or a club with a skull at the top, considered as the weapon of Śiva (and carried by ascetics and Jogīs); one of the attributes of Śiva.

def: s.f. 'Bed-sickness'; taking to bed in the sulks or dumps; feigning sickness
subs: To take to bed in the dumps, to feign sickness.

def: s.m. A dunghill, dust-heap.
s.m. = khatwārā, q.v.
s.f. 'Bed-sickness'; taking to bed in the sulks or dumps; feigning sickness
subs: To take to bed in the dumps, to feign sickness.

def: s.f. 'Bed-sickness'; taking to bed in the sulks or dumps; feigning sickness
subs: To take to bed in the dumps, to feign sickness.

ety: khaṭā or khaṭṭā, q.v.+S. आलः, with euphonic y
def: adj. (dialec.) Sour, cross, crabbed, morose, surly, gruff, harsh.
s.m. corr. of qat̤-zan, q.v.s.v. qat̤.
s.m.f. Eating; that eats; voracious, gluttonous; (met.) that takes, or receives; that sustains or undergoes, &c.; — a great eater, a glutton; one who takes or receives (bribes)
subs: adj. & s.m.f. Extravagant, prodigal; — an extravagant person, &c.

def: s.f. A cave, cavern, den (of a wild beast), chasm (in a mountain); a pit, hole (in the ground); a subterranean vault or storehouse (for grain, &c.).
s.m. corr. of khoṅdhā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of خاف, for خوف 'to fear'
def: s.m. Fear, dread, terror
subs: ḵẖauf dikhānā, v.t. To impress (one) with fear (of, -kā), to frighten, terrify; to threaten
To evince fear (in, -meṅ); to be afraid (of, -se), to fear
(dil-meṅ), To give place to fear (in the heart)
v.t. To put into a state of fear, to frighten, to intimidate
adj. Frightful, terrifying; — afraid, frightened, terrified
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَوف (xawf), from Arabic خَوْف (ḵawf).

def: s.m. (dialec.) Scale of a fish, &c.); quill (of a porcupine)
s.m. (dialec.) Scale of a fish, &c.); quill (of a porcupine)
s.f. The act of boiling.
v.t. To cause to boil; to boil (water, &c.), to make boiling hot.
s.f. (Dakkhinī) The sole (fish), Pleuronectes solea.

ety: khaul˚ = S. क्ष्वेड(ति), rt. क्ष्विड्; or S. क्ष्वेल(ति), rt. क्ष्वेल्
def: v.n. To boil, to be agitated by heat; — to burn (mentally).
part. adj. (f. -ī), Boiling; boiling hot.
s.f. = anjalī, q.v.
s.f. Coughing; sound of coughing
s.m. An old slipper or shoe.
s.f. The tilak or (crescent-shaped) mark made on the forehead (of Hindūs, worshippers of Śiva) with sandal-wood, saffron, &c.
s.m. The rot (among sheep); the falling off of the hairs.
s.m. Scraping or pawing the ground with the hoof (as an ox); pawing the ground and bellowing
subs: To scrape or paw the ground with the hoof
(-se), To commence pawing the ground and bellowing; to bellow defiantly (at).

ety: inf. n. of خوض 'to wade; to enter'
def: s.m. Diving (into), entering; considering; consulting; purposing, resolving, intending; consideration, attention; deep thought; research
subs: (-meṅ), To dive (into), enter (into), go deep (into), to consider deeply or with attention; to be lost in thought or meditation.

def: s.f. Manure, &c. ( = khāt or khad, qq.v.).
s.m. The shoulder; shoulder-blade.
s.m. An eater ( = khāne-wālā).
v.t. 'To cause to eat,' to feed, &c. ( = khilānā, q.v.).
s.m. Lord, master; husband
subs: To take a husband, to marry.

def: s.m. pl. (of P. ḵẖān), Lords, nobles, &c.
s.m. The west; (but often used by poets for) the east; — the sun
subs: s.m. 'The monarch of the east,' the sun.

def: s.m. pl. (of ḵẖāwar), The east and west; — name of a district in Ḵẖurāsān.
adj. Of or belonging to Ḵẖāwarān, q.v.
adj. Western; eastern; — solar.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖārijī), Heretics, schismatics.
s.m. Events infringing the usual course of nature, unusual things, miracles.

ety: the arabicized form of P. ḵẖawarna; Old P. ḵẖvarna, fr. Z. hvare = S. svar
def: s.m. A magnificent edifice, a palace; the lofty palace built by Nǒʻmān-bin-Munẕir for king Bahrām Gor, in Babylonia.
s.f. The office or duty of an attendant; — the place where one sits behind a great man upon an elephant; the back seat in a hauda, &c
subs: v.n. To sit in attendance behind a great man on an elephant.

ety: of ḵẖāṣṣ, 'peculiar,' &c. and of ḵẖāṣṣa, 'distinguished person'
def: s.f. Bidding for the seat of honour (in the car which bears the Jain god; cf. ḵẖawāṣī)
subs: s.m. The apartments or habitation allotted to the domestics (of a great man); the servants' quarters
s.m. pl. Nobles and plebeians, high and low ( = ḵẖaṣṣ-o-ʻām).

def: s.m. pl. (of خاصة 'a property'), Properties, qualities, attributes
s.f. = khāṭ, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of ḵẖātūn, q.v.), Ladies.
s.m. (f.?), A green field, a sown field, a field of green wheat or barley; green wheat or barley cut for fodder ( = vulg. ḵẖūd).
adv. In the manner of a lord or master, like a master, lordly.
s.f. Dominion, mastership; protection, patronage; favour, kindness.
s.m. An egg; a testicle
subs: s.m. A cringing or obsequious fellow, a fawner
v.t. To disregard, treat with contempt; to be disobedient, be stubborn, not to submit to obedience
(-kā), To curry favour (with); to cringe, &c. ( = ḵẖāya-bardārī karnā)

ety: v.n. fr. خال, for خيل 'to think, to fancy,' &c.
def: s.m. Thought, opinion, surmise, suspicion, conception, idea, notion, fancy, imagination, conceit. whim, chimera; consideration; regard, deference; apprehension; care, concern; — an imaginary form, apparition, vision, spectre, phantom, shadow, delusion; — a kind of song
subs: s.m. Foolish imagination; a wrong idea, false notion, misconception
(-kā), To form a conception or idea (of), to imagine; to frame a thought or image in the mind; to build castles in the air; — to arrange (one's) thoughts
s.f. Structure of the imagination; — imagining, dreaming, castle-building
adj. & s.m. Fanciful; — a visionary; a castle-builder
v.n. To occur (to, -ko), to seem; — to give the mind (to, -par), fix the thoughts (on), to pursue (an object)
see ḵẖayālī pulāʼo pakānā, s.v. ḵẖayālī
(-kā), To relinquish all thought (of); to be out of conceit (with)
s.m. A crude notion; a vain or ridiculous idea
v.t. To bear in mind; — ḵẖayāl-se bāhar, adj. Inconceivable
s.m. A false notion, vicious idea; idle fancy; — corrupt or lewd thoughts
(-ko, or kā), To think (of), to consider, imagine, fancy, conceive; to regard attentively, to attend (to), direct the attention (to), to mind, observe; to form designs (against, -kā)
v.n. To be constantly, or always, thinking (of a thing)
(-ko), To give no thought to, pay no regard to, not to take into consideration, to disregard; to consider as unworthy of thought; to show no consideration or respect for
(with dat., -ko, of pers., and gen., -kā, of thing), To lose all recollection (of), to forget (about), to escape (one's) memory
v.t., 1˚ = ḵẖayāl-meṅ na lānā, q.v.; — 2˚, to think nothing of, to pass (offensive speech, &c.) unnoticed, not to take amiss
adv. In consideration (of, -ke), with regard (to), with reference (to); out of consideration or regard (for).

def: s.m. Apparition, spectre, phantom, &c. ( = ḵẖayāl, q.v.).
s.m. Vain thoughts, absurd ideas, ill-grounded expectations.
adj. Ideal, imaginary; fanciful, capricious; fantastical; visionary; chimerical
subs: To indulge in vain speculations or absurd fancies; to build castles in the air.

def: s.f. A kind of violet.
s.m.(?) Pottery, earthen vessel or vessels; potsherd; broken shells.
s.m.(?) = ḵẖazaf, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖizāna), Repositories; treasuries, &c.
s.m. A treasurer, cash-keeper

ety: act. part. n. of خزن 'to store,' &c.
def: s.m. A treasurer, keeper or guardian (of property), custodian.
s.m. A treasury, &c. ( = ḵẖazāna, q.v.).
s.f. A coarse kind of silk (cf. P. qaz).

r: ḵẖe, ḵẖāʼě-manqūt̤a, ḵẖā-ě-mǒʻjama, ch, loch, abjad
def: the tenth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the seventh of the Arabic, from which it is borrowed), called ḵẖāʼě-manqūt̤a, and ḵẖā-ě-mǒʻjama. It is one of the faucial or guttural letters, and has no corresponding sound in English, but is approximately expressed by the Scottish pronunciation of ch in the word loch. As a numeral (see abjad) it denotes 600; — in astronomy it denotes the planet Mars.

r: khe-ćar, khai-ćar, khe, kha, ćara, lit.
ety: khe, loc. of kha+ćara, lit. 'moving in the sky'
def: s.m. (f.-ī), A bird; a planet; a vidyādhar, or kind of demi-god; genii.
corr. form of khiṅćnā, q.v. (and cf. kheṅćnā).
s.m. Lassitude, depression, despondency; faintness; fatigue; pain; regret, sorrow, grief, affliction, trouble, distress; — complaint, disorder, ailment
subs: (-ko), To give pain (to), to grieve, distress, &c.
(-kā), To grieve (about), to bewail, regret; — to be distressing or injurious (to); to be disagreeable or obnoxious (to)

def: s.m. The trap or enclosure in which elephants are caught; — a strong bird (or other animal) which beats and drives away other birds, &c.
adj. Of or relating to a khedā or elephant-trap
subs: s.m. Expenses incurred in catching wild elephants; a cess levied for that purpose.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Grieving, sorrowing, regretting, regretful, repentant ( = khedit, q.v.).
part. & s.m. (f. -ā), Disturbed, annoyed, harassed; distressed, afflicted, pained, grieved; sorrowful, repentant; — a distressed person; a sorrowful person, &c.

ety: khed˚ = S. खिन्ट्(ति), rt. खिद्; or = S. खेद
def: v.n. = khed karnā, q.v.s.v. khed.

ety: khed˚ = S. खेदय(ति), caus. of rt. खिद्
def: v.t. To run after, to persecute; to hunt, chase, chevy, drive away.
s.f. Dry brambles; — a hedge of brambles or thorns.
s.m. = kekṛā or keṅkṛā, q.v.
s.f. A thin crisp cake.
s.m. A mark, spot, sign; a mark (in the body) caused by pressure.
s.m. (dialec.) A troublesome affair or business, an embarrassment, encumbrance, bore, nuisance; — hardship, misfortune, calamity, epidemic disease, plague, pest, &c.
subs: A hitch or embarrassment to arise; a misfortune or calamity to occur.

ety: Prk. खेड्डं or खेल्लं; S. क्रीडं
def: s.m. Sport, play, fun, frolic, pastime, amusement; a game; — amorous sport; sexual intercourse; — a wonder (of nature), a sight or spectacle (syn. tamāshā)
subs: (-kā), To spoil the sport (of); to spoil (one's) business, or game
(-ko), To regard as child's play, to consider very easy
s.m. Skipping and dancing, play and sport, gambols
adj. & s.f. Playsome; wanton; — an unchaste woman, an adulteress, a strumpet
(-ko), To play with (a person, or with a fish); to lead (one) a dance, to worry, harass
To play a game; to play
To invent a game, or trick, &c.

def: s.m. A trough (for watering cattle).
adj. & s.f. Playful; unsteady, wanton; foolish, silly; careless, untidy slovenly (woman or girl); — a playful, or foolish woman, &c.
part. adj. & s.f. Experienced, knowing (woman); — an unchaste woman, a harlot.
v.t. = khilānā, q.v.
s.m. Unsteadiness; foolishness; slovenliness, &c. (see ḵẖelā).
adj. & s.m. = khilāṛī, q.v.
part. (dialec.) Playing, sporting, gambolling; playful, sportive.
s.m. prop. khilaunā, q.v.

ety: Prk. खेल्लणअं; S. क्रीडनीयं, rt. क्रीड्
def: v.n. To play, sport, gambol, frisk, frolic; to amuse or divert oneself; — to play at; to gamble; to play a part or character, to act, perform; — to nod the head under the influence of a spirit
subs: (intens.), To play out (one's part, or life), to die.

def: v.t. = khilwānā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = khilwāṛ, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = khilwāṛ, q.v.
s.m. = kshem, q.v.
s.m. Name of a measure in Hindī versification.
v.t. = kheʼonā, q.v.
s.f. = khinḍṛī, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = keṅkṛā or kekṛā, q.v.
v.t. (dialec.) To pass over, cross, traverse; — to move out of the way, push aside; — to turn over (grain, &c.).

s.f. lit. 'A throw, or cast' (as of a load of stone, &c.); a journey to and from, a trip, voyage; a turn, a single time or occasion; a load; cargo; shipment; an assortment; periodical supply (of grain or merchandize); — a piece of base metal inserted in a coin; (in Alg.) something added to a sum, additive quantity, addendum; — an apartment
subs: or khep lādnā, To load; to carry a load (to), to perform a trip or voyage
To lose on a single time or occasion; to suffer a loss.

def: adj. & s.m. (dialec.) (f. -ī), Distracted, foolish, mad, insane; — a madman; an idiot, a fool.

ety: khep, q.v.+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. One who makes a trip; a voyager.

ety: khep˚ = Prk. खिप्प(इ) or खिप्पे(इ) = S. क्षिप्य(ति), rt. क्षिप्
def: v.t. To get rid of; to pass, to spend (time).
s.m. A mixture, &c. ( = kheṛā, q.v.).

ety: S. क्षेत्र+कः; and खेटकः, prob. a Prk. form
def: s.m. (dialec.) A field, land ( = khet); — a small village, hamlet; — a mound of ruins, the site of an ancient town or village; — mixture, mixedness; a mixture (esp. of corn or grain)
subs: s.m. Headman of a village; — a Brāhman entitled to perform certain religious ceremonies in a village and to receive the fees appertaining thereto.

def: s.m. Line; furrow; wrinkle.
s.m. (Dakh.) = kheṛyālū, q.v.
s.f. = kherī, q.v.
s.f. A kind of steel (said to be so called from the town of Kheṛī).

ety: prob. Prk. खिरिआ; S. क्षर+इका; cf. khernā
def: s.f. Afterbirth; the membranes in which the fœtus is enveloped, secundines.
s.f. = kherī, q.v.
s.f. A kind of steel (said to be so called from the town of Kheṛī).

ety: Prk. खिरणअं; S. क्षरणीयं, rt. क्षर्
def: v.n. To run, flow; to run or pass (through); to pass away.
s.m. (Dakh.) Husbandman, peasant ( = khet-wālā).
s.m. = khes, q.v.
s.m. A kind of figured cloth; diaper, damask; — a sheet or wrapper of such cloth.
s.m. A kind of figured cloth (prob. = khes, q.v.).
s.f. = khisārī, q.v.
s.m. Ground, land, soil; a field; field (of battle); (in Geom.) a plain superficies, a surface; — a sacred spot or district, a holy place; — origin, breed, caste
subs: v.n. To be slain in the field (of battle); to become helpless
or khet-ḵẖat, s.m. Mortgage of a field
To flee from the field (of battle)
s.m. The owner or occupant of a field
v.t. To kill in the field (of battle)
khet rahnā 'To remain in the field
To cultivate, till (land); — to begin to rise, to appear (the moon)
To prepare the ground or soil, to manure land
or khet nikānā, or khet marānā, To weed a field
adv. According to the fields; field by field; per field.

def: s.m. Hunting, sport, the chase.
s.f.m. A shield; — a mace, the club of Balarām: — a village.
s.m. A sportsman, a hunter.
s.f. Husbandry, agriculture, tillage, farming; — corn-field; sown-land; a crop; produce; — adj. (S. क्षेत्रिकः) Arable
subs: adj. Fit for cultivation, culturable, arable
To practise or engage in husbandry, to till (the ground)

def: s.m. (rustic) = khet, q.v.
s.m. Crossing (a river); — a boat; — a ferry(?)
subs: To put or row a boat across (a stream).

def: s.m. Price of passage in a boat, passage-money, ferry-money, fare.

r: khewaiyā, khiwaiyā, khew, nā, waiya, kahwaiyā
def: s.m. = khewak, or khewaṭiyā, q.v.
s.m. A rower, boatman.
s.f. Assigned share of revenue; — record or register of shares into which a coparcenary village is divided; register of mutations; — administration paper
subs: s.m. Holder of a share in a coparcenary village
s.f. Register or account of village-management; a record showing the distribution of lands and rights of the occupants.

ety: Prk. केवट्टो, and केवट्टओ; S.कैवर्तः, and कैवर्तिकः
def: s.m. 'A fisherman,' waterman, boat-man, rower (cf. next).

ety: Prk. केवट्टो, and केवट्टओ; S.कैवर्तः, and कैवर्तिकः
def: s.m. 'A fisherman,' waterman, boat-man, rower (cf. next).

ety: khew˚ = Prk. खिव्व(इ) = S. क्षिप्य(ति), rt. क्षिप्
def: v.t. To row, punt, paddle; — to endure, suffer (pain, trouble, &c.); — to serve, prove helpful or useful to (one, in time of need).
s.m. Rising; springing up; leaping, bounding, capering; raising, exciting, producing; — riser; raiser; &c. (used in comp., e.g. ṣubḥ-ḵẖez; fitna-ḵẖez, qq.v.); — s.m.(?), A rising; a spring, leap, &c.
part. adj. Rising; springing up (as flowers, &c.).
s.f. Rising, &c. (used in comp.).
s.f. Rising; springing up, &c.

ety: for A. خبرة, v.n. fr. خبر 'to know,' &c.
def: s.f. Wisdom, learning; experience; experiment, trial, proof, test.
s.m. Aversion, dislike, repugnance, reluctance.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Drawn, pulled; attracted; drawn out, extended, stretched; drawn tight, tightened; tight, tense; attenuated, slender, fine; — drawn (as a figure), delineated, sketched
s.f. Drawing, dragging, pulling, tugging, &c. ( = khićāʼo); — price paid for drawing, or pulling ( = khićwāʼī).
v.t. To cause to pull or draw; to cause to stretch or tighten, &c., &c.; — to pull, draw; to stretch, &c. ( = khīṅćnā, q.v.).

ety: khićā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ) तव्यं; — and ˚āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Drawing, pulling, dragging; attracting, attraction; drawing out, stretching; tightening; — drag, pull, draught; tightness, tenseness; tension; strain; firmness; cohesion; — discord, strife, contention.

s.f. A dish made of rice and split pulse (dāl) boiled together, with ghī and spices; — a mixture, medley, hodge-podge, jumble; — a barbarous mixture of two languages, a lingua franca, a jargon; — miscellaneous charges or taxes; — a mixture of black and grey hairs; — a mixed heap of gold and silver coin; — earnest money given to a dancing-girl (when she is engaged for an entertainment); — the flower of the ber-tree, Zizyphus jujuba
subs: To cook khićṛīʼ; to concoct schemes or plans, to scheme, plan, plot; to take counsel together
A plan to be concocted, a plot to be hatched
or kar-denā, v.t. To make a jumble or a mess of; to mar, spoil
v.n. To be jumbled or confused, to be made a mess of, to be marred or spoilt; — to be or become sprinkled with grey (the hair).

ety: khićā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ) तव्यं; — and ˚āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Drawing, pulling, dragging; attracting, attraction; drawing out, stretching; tightening; — drag, pull, draught; tightness, tenseness; tension; strain; firmness; cohesion; — discord, strife, contention.
v.t. = khīṅćnā, q.v.

v.n. To be drawn, dragged, or pulled, &c.; to be attracted; to be absorbed, be sucked in; to be drawn out, be extended, be stretched; to stretch; to be extracted; to be drawn tight, be tightened; — to draw aside or away (from), to hold or keep aloof (from), to fight shy (of); — to be drawn, be delineated, be sketched, be traced; — to be borne, be endured, or suffered; — v.t. To draw, &c. ( = khīṅćnā, q.v.)
s.m. A dish made of rice, barley (or wheat) and various sorts of pulse, &c. boiled together (see next).
s.f. Cost of drawing, or extracting, or delineating, &c.
v.t. To cause to be drawn or pulled, &c.; to cause to be stretched, or tightened, &c. (see khićnā)
v.t. (dialec.) = khadeṛnā, q.v.

r: ḵẖidīv, ḵẖidev, ḵẖudev, ḵẖadev, ḵẖideʼo, ḵẖẉud, ḵẖudā
def: s.m. Lord, master; an independent ruler, a king, sovereign, a mighty monarch; Khedive (as of Egypt); the unequalled of the age.

ety: for A. خدمة, inf. or v.n. of خدم 'to serve'
def: s.f. Service, attendance, ministry; employment, business, office, appointment, function, duty; use
subs: (-kī), To serve, wait (on), minister (to); (fig.), to serve out, to beat; to satirize
A serving-man, a servant, a table-servant; a butler
s.f. Service, ministry, attendance; readiness to serve
adj. & s.m. Ready to serve, forward, obliging; — a willing or faithful servant
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خِدْمَت (xidmat), borrowed from Arabic خِدْمَة (ḵidma), from خَدَمَ (ḵadama). First attested in c. 1603 as Middle Hindi خدمت (xdmt /⁠xidmat⁠/).

def: s.f. pl. (of ḵẖidmat), Services; situations, appointments.
adj. & s.m. Employed; official, ministerial; — s.m. A servant, an attendant (esp. at a mosque).
adj. = ḵẖafīf, q.v.

ety: for A. خفة, inf. n. of خفّ 'to be light'
def: s.f. Lightness, levity, want of dignity; — indignity, abasement, humiliation, disgrace; affront, slight
subs: v.n. To suffer indignity, slight, or disgrace, &c.
(-kī), To treat with indignity, &c.; to slight; to vilify, to slander.

def: s.f. An alkaline earth, fossil alkali (syn. reh, sajjī).
s.f. Vexation, irritation, spleen; teasing; fretfulness
subs: or khij pakaṛnā, v.n. To become vexed or irritated, to take offence; to become fretful; to take to teasing
To vent (one's) spleen (on); — to mock, scoff (at), jeer.

def: s.f. Anger, vexation, irritation, spleen; fretfulness; — teasing (see khij).

ety: caus. of khījnā; — khijā = Prk. खिज्जावे˚ = S. खिद+आपय˚, rt. खिद्
def: v.t. To vex, irritate, tease, annoy, provoke, excite, enrage; to trouble, disturb, molest, pain.
s.f. Vexation, irritation ( = khij).
s.f. = khij, q.v.
v.t. = khijānā, q.v.; — v.n. = khījnā, q.v.
v.t. & v.n. = khijhānā, q.v.
v.n. = khījnā, q.v.

ety: khīj˚ or khījh˚ = Prk. किज्ज(इ) = S. खिद्य(ते), rt. खिद्
def: v.n. To be vexed, be teased; to be or become irritated or angry; to fret.
v.t. To vex, &c. ( = khijānā, q.v.); — v.n. To get angry; to be fretful or irritable ( = khijnā).
v.n. = khījnā, q.v.

ety: khīj˚ or khījh˚ = Prk. किज्ज(इ) = S. खिद्य(ते), rt. खिद्
def: v.n. To be vexed, be teased; to be or become irritated or angry; to fret.
s.f. A leathern water-bottle or bag.
s.m. A fox (syn. lomṛī).
s.m. = kīl, q.v.
s.f. A bolt, pin, &c. (see kīl); a small or minute particle, a bit
subs: v.n. To be broken to pieces.

def: s.f. Parched grain or rice (inflated or puffed out so as to appear like froth; — syn. khoʼī, lāwā).
part. (f. -ī), Opened, expanded, blown (as a flower); — rejoiced

ety: inf. n. of iii of خلف 'to succeed or follow,' &c.
def: s.m. Contrary, opposite (of, -kā); opposition, contrariety; hostility, enmity; the contrary of truth, falsehood; — postpn. In opposition (to, -ke), contrary (to), adverse (to), against, (as the first member of compounds, it may sometimes be conveniently rendered by the English prefixes un-, mis-, &c.)
subs: Opposed to authority, not authorized, unwarranted
s.f. Contradictory statement, contradiction; prevarication; misrepresentation
Against the will or disposition (of); unnatural
To make a contradictory or false statement, to tell a falsehood; to misrepresent
Contrary to the will (of), against the wish (of)
(in Med.) Disagreeing with the habit of the body
(-kī), To distort or pervert the meaning (of); to misrepresent
s.f. A cross suit or action; a counter-charge
(-kī), To oppose; to contravene; to commit an offence
Contrary to good manners or to custom; unnatural
Contrary to promise or agreement; — s.m. A promise-breaker
(-ke), To be opposed (to), to be against

ety: for A. خلافة, inf. n. of خلف 'to succeed or follow'
def: s.f. Deputy-ship, lieutenancy; the office or dignity of Caliph (khalīfa); — imperial dignity, monarchy (especially the succession of princes who ruled at Damascus and Bagdad from the time of Mohammad to that of Hulāku Khān).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خلافت (xilāfat), from Arabic خِلَافَة (ḵilāfa).

def: s.f. Feeding; — a dry nurse, a nurse
s.m. = khelne-wālā, khilāṛī, and khillī-bāz, q.v.s.v. khillī.
s.m. (orig.) 'A pin; a skewer,' &c.; a tooth-pick; a long thorn or prickle, straw, &c. (such being often used as a pin and as a toothpick)
subs: ḵẖilāl karnā (-meṅ), To use a toothpick.

ety: prob. pl. of ḵẖalal; but used grammatically as a sing. syn. with ḵẖalal, q.v.
def: s.m. Intervening space, interval, middle; — imperfection, defect, flaw, damage, ruin; disorder; rout, overthrow, defeat
subs: ḵẖilāl karnā, v.t. To throw into disorder; to overthrow, to defeat.

ety: caus. of khelnā; — khilā˚ = Prk. खेल्लावे˚ = S. क्रीड+आपय˚, caus. augment; rt. क्रीड्
def: v.t. To cause to play; to allow to play; — to play with; to dandle; to amuse. divert (a child).

v.t. To cause to eat; to give food to, to feed; to entertain; to cause to take (as medicine, or the air, or an oath, &c.); to give, to administer (poison, &c.); to cause to suffer or undergo (as a beating, &c.)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Who feeds; — feeder, supporter, sustainer
s.f. A woman who supports her lover.

def: v.t. To cause to blossom, or blow; cause to expand, &c.

ety: khiland = Prk. खेल्लंतो = S. क्रीडन्+ṛā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Playful, playsome, frolicsome, &c. ( = khillū, and khilwāṛ).
v.t. (dialec.) = khilānā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = khilāṛī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = khilāṛī, q.v.
s.f. A playful, or a wanton woman; a coquette ( = khelnī).

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Fond of, or addicted to, play; playful, playsome, gamesome, sportive; jocund, facetious; full of tricks, mischievous; — skilful at play; addicted to gaming or gambling; — skilled in juggling or conjuring; — one who is fond of play or sport; a sportive man or boy; an adept at any game; — a trickster; — a gamester, gambler; — a juggler.
s.f. pl. Sports, gambols, tricks
subs: To gambol; to practise or perform tricks (as a dog, &c.).

ety: khilāṛ, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Playfulness, sportiveness, wantonness, &c.
s.m.f. Giving to eat, feeding, who feeds; — feeder, supporter ( = khilāne-wālā); — (fr. khilānā, 'to cause to bloom'), adj. Causing to bloom, &c.
s.m. A thing to amuse, a plaything, a toy; — a bawble, a gewgaw; — a buffoca, mimic, jester.

ety: i.q. khilwāṛ, or Prk. खेल्ला+उल्लो = S. क्रीडा+उलः
def: adj. & s.m. = khilāṛī, q.v.

ety: khīl = khīlī, q.v. + Prk. अउल्लिआ = S. क+उल+इका
def: s.f. = khīlī, and gilaurī, qq.v.
adj. (rustic) = khilwāṛ, q.v.

ety: S. कुहलि+का; or khīl = kīl,+S. इका
def: s.f. Betel-leaf made up with the different ingredients, a flake of pān (syn. bīṛā, gilaurī).
s.f. Creation; constitution, natural constitution; the creation, the world; people, populace
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خِلْقَت (xilqat), from Arabic خِلْقَة (ḵilqa).

def: s.f. Tittering, giggling; a burst of laughter.
v.n. To burst out laughing, to laugh heartily or aloud; to giggle, titter; — to shake or rattle together
subs: To laugh aloud, to burst out laughing.

def: adj. Natural, constitutional, innate, inherent.
s.m. = ḵẖalīl, q.v.
s.f. = khīlī, q.v. (and cf. gilaurī).

ety: Prk. खेड्डिआ or खेल्लिआ = S. क्रीड+इका
def: s.f. Fun, jest, joke, humour, sally of wit
subs: To make fun, to jest, to poke fun (at); to make a fool (of)
adj. & s.m. Playsome, humorous, funny, jocular, jocose; — a jocular fellow, a wag, &c.
s.f. Play, fun, jest; humour, jocularity; — flirtation
To make fun, to jest, joke; — to flirt
(-ko), To pass off as a joke; — to make fun of, make a laughing-stock of.

def: s.m.f. Playful, playsome, frelicsome, jocund, humorous, facetious, funny; pleasant, entertaining; — a playful person; a humorous person, &c.

ety: khil˚ = Prk. खिड्ड(इ) or खिल्ल(इ) = S. क्रीड्य(ते), pass. of rt. क्रीड्
def: v.n. To open, expand (as a flower), to blow, bloom, flower; to open, crack, burst, swell (as a wall, or plaster, or parched grain, &c.; cf. khīl); to break out, show itself or its effects (as intoxicating liquor, &c.); — to be set off (by), to show to advantage (on, -par), to look well or becoming (as a dress or a person, or one colour upon another); — to expand or swell (with pleasure), to be exhilarated, be delighted; to rejoice, laugh
s.m. One of the four humours of the human body; — (as a plur.) constitution, nature
subs: s.m. Corrupt humour; — canker of a sore, Cacochymia.

ety: for A. خلطة ḵẖilt̤aṭ, fr. خلط 'to mix'
def: s.m. Social or familiar intercourse; enjoyment of society and the pleasures of life; conviviality.
v.t. To cause (someone) to feed (someone else), to cause to be fed, to have or get (one) fed.
v.t. To cause to be played with; to cause to be amused or diverted.
adj. = khillū, q.v. (cf. also khilāṛ and khilāṛī).
s.f. Forgiveness, pardon, absolution (see kshamā; and cf. ćhimā).
s.m. corr. of قيمه qīma, q.v.
adj. = kshīn, q.v.
part. Rubbed, worn, &c.
s.f. (dialec.) = ćhināl, q.v.
s.f. Drawing, pulling; a pull.
part. adj. = khićā, q.v.
s.f. = H کهنچاو खिंचाव khiṅćāʼo, s.m. = H کهنچاوٿ खिंचावट khiṅćāwaṭ, s.f. See khićāʼo, and khićāwaṭ.
s.f. = khićāʼī, q.v.
v.t. = khićānā, q.v.
s.m. = H کهنچاوٿ खिंचावट khiṅćāwaṭ, s.f. = H کهنچاهٿ खिंचाहट khiṅćāhaṭ, s.f. See khićāʼo, and khićāwaṭ.
s.f. = H کهنچاو खिंचाव khiṅćāʼo, s.m. = H کهنچاهٿ खिंचाहट khiṅćāhaṭ, s.f. See khićāʼo, and khićāwaṭ.
v.n. = khićnā, q.v.
v.t. = khićwānā, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.), A hole, perforation; an opening, fissure, chink, gap, break; a notch, a jag (on the edge of a knife, &c).
v.t. To scatter, strew; to waste, to dissipate (wealth, &c.)

ety: prob. a var. form of khanḍnā; cf. khinḍ
def: v.n. To be scattered or dispersed; to be spread broadcast; to be wasted or dissipated.
v.n. = khinḍnā, q.v.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) Patched or ragged cloak (of a mendicant); a patched quilt, or a quilt stuffed with cotton (syn. nihālī).

ety: khind, prob. = khand = S. खण्ड+dim. aff. ṛī = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A patched or ragged garment (syn. gudṛī); — a baby's quilt; — a pallet, bedding (of a beggar).
v.t. To cause to be scattered, &c.
s.m. A white, or grey, or cream-coloured, or dappled horse; a horse, steed.
adj. & s.m. Able-bodied; — an athletic clown.
v.n. To speak or cry snuffily, to snivel, to whimper, pule, fret.
s.m. A ship in ballast, empty ship; the hold of a ship.

ety: khin˚ = Prk. खीण = S. क्षीण, rt. क्षि
def: v.n. To be wasted or worn; to be rubbed; to wear out.
part. Depressed, distressed, suffering pain or uneasiness; fatigued, wearied, exhausted.
s.f. = khirnī, s.f.
s.f. The little finger; the little toe.

ety: v.n. fr. خزر 'to be narrow and small' (the eye
def: s.m. Hog, pig, swine.
adj. Hoggish, swinish; — s.f. A hog-sty.
s.f. Rice-milk, a dish made of rice and milk (rice, parboiled in water, is again boiled in milk, with the addition of sugar, and sometimes spices and kernels)
subs: s.f. Giving a child khīr to taste for the first time, weaning (this is done when the child is about six or seven months old); the ceremonies and festivites observed on such an occasion
'To be changed from khīr to coarse meal': to suffer a reverse of fortune; to be ruined or marred (a hope, or aim).

def: s.m. A cucumber, Cucumis sativus, or C. utilissimus.
adj. Obscure, dark; black-hearted, wicked, malevolent, malignant; vain; head-strong, froward; — dazzled; stupefied; astonished, confounded.

ety: old P. khard; Pehl. khart; Zend khratu; S. kratu
def: s.f. Understanding, judgment, intellect, wisdom
subs: adj. Approved by the understanding, commendable
ḵẖirad-war, adj. Possessing understanding, intelligent, sagacious, wise

def: s.f. Darkness, obscurity, dimness; the state of being dazzled, or of having the sight overcome; dizziness; stupefaction; astonishment; — blackness of heart, malevolence, malignity (cf. tīragī).
s.m. (Dakkhinī) = kheṛyālū, q.v.
s.f. Pace, gait, walk, march; stately gait, graceful walk; strut
subs: adj. Walking elegantly, or with a stately gait.

def: part. Walking in a stately manner, moving gracefully; strutting
subs: adv. With a graceful or stately movement.

def: s.f. Graceful or stately walking.
s.f. (in comp.) Walking, gait.
s.f. A black ring (painted on a tambourine).
s.m. (dialec.) A toothpick (syn. kharkā).
s.f. The gilliflower (particularly the yellow species); a species of violet, Viola alba (and other species); Althæa rosea.
s.f. Udder; breast, pap.
v.n. To be scattered; to be dashed out (the brains).

ety: for A. خرقة ḵẖirqat, fem.; fr. خرق 'to tear,' &c.
def: s.m. A ragged, patched garment; dress of a devotee or religious mendicant
subs: s.m. One who wears a ragged garment, a devotee, a religious mendicant.

def: s.f. A dialec. form of khiṛkī, q.v.
s.f. A private or back-door; postern-gate, wicket, sally-port; a window, casement; a shutter; an opening (in front of an angarkhā, or a turban, &c.)
subs: adj. Having a window or casement, &c.; having an opening on the breast or in front (an angarkhā, or a turban).

def: s.f. = khiṛkī, q.v.
s.f. = khiṛkī, q.v.
s.m. Harvest; heap, stack, or rick of unthreshed corn; a barn
s.f. The tree Mimusops kauki, and its fruit ( = khīrī).
s.m. A bear
subs: s.f. Bear's play, rough play, rudeness, romping.
wety: PIE word *h₂ŕ̥tḱos From Classical Persian خرس (xirs, “bear”).

def: s.m. A dish (commonly curds) made with biestings.
s.f. = khīs, q.v.
s.f. Biestings (syn. peʼosī).
s.f. Grinning, showing the teeth, chattering (as a monkey); a grin (of rage, mortification, &c.), shrug, grimace; — loss, waste
subs: v.n. To put on a grin, to grin

def: s.m. A purse; a small bag; a pocket; — a rough glove, or rubber (used in baths).

ety: khis˚ = khas˚ = Prk. खसक्के(इ) = prob. S. खच+कृ
def: v.n. To stir, move, slip; to move or slip out of place, be displaced, to shift; to slip away, steal away, slink away, sheer off, decamp, abscond
s.f. pl. (of ḵẖaṣlat, q.v.), Qualities; habits good qualities, talents, virtues, &c.
v.n. To slip, slide (cf. phisalnā); — to incline or be drawn (to), to lean (towards); — adj. (f. -ī), Slippery
subs: v.n. (intens.) To slip; to slip down.

ety: prob. fr. S. क्लिश्; cf. khisiyānā and khisaknā
def: v.n. To move or get aside, move out of the way; to draw back (from), shrink (from), flinch (from); to be abashed or ashamed
subs: (-se), To remain apart (from), hold aloof (from); to draw back (from), shrink (from), to flinch; to be abashed.

ety: fr. ḵẖisāndan; rt. Zend kar = S. kar, fr. kr̤ī
def: s.m. An infusion (in medicine).
s.f. A kind of pulse, Lathyrus sativus ( = kisārī).
part. Moved aside, &c.
s.f. corr. of ḵẖishtak, q.v.

ety: Pehl. khastak; Zend istya; S. ishṭakā
def: s.f. A brick; a tile
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خِشت (xišt, “brick”), doublet of اِیْنٹ (ī̃ṭ). Ultimately derived from the BMAC substrate. Cognate with Punjabi اِٹّ (īṭṭ), Pashto خښته (ḵẖaś̱ẖtaʿh), Khowar اݰٹو (ušṭu), Palula اݜٹو (iṣṭū́),

def: s.f. A small brick; — a square piece of cloth put under the arm-pit of a shirt, &c., a gusset; cod-piece; — drawers, trousers.
adj. Of brick; of tile, tiled.
s.f. dim. of khīsā, q.v.

ety: khisiyān, for khisiyānā; ˚pat and ˚paṭ, prob. fr. S. vr̤itti; ˚pan and ˚panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚āhaṭ, as in khislāhaṭ, q.v.
def: The state of being put to shame, mortification, humiliation; vexation; — shamefacedness, bashfulness; — peevishness, fretfulness.

v.n. To be put to shame, to be abashed, to be mortified; to grin (with mortification, &c.), to gnash the teeth (with rage); to look blank or foolish; — adj. Abashed, put to shame, mortified, humiliated; disconcerted, disappointed; ready to cry; — peevish, fretful

ety: khisiyān, for khisiyānā; ˚pat and ˚paṭ, prob. fr. S. vr̤itti; ˚pan and ˚panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚āhaṭ, as in khislāhaṭ, q.v.
def: The state of being put to shame, mortification, humiliation; vexation; — shamefacedness, bashfulness; — peevishness, fretfulness.

ety: khisiyān, for khisiyānā; ˚pat and ˚paṭ, prob. fr. S. vr̤itti; ˚pan and ˚panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚āhaṭ, as in khislāhaṭ, q.v.
def: The state of being put to shame, mortification, humiliation; vexation; — shamefacedness, bashfulness; — peevishness, fretfulness.

ety: khisiyān, for khisiyānā; ˚pat and ˚paṭ, prob. fr. S. vr̤itti; ˚pan and ˚panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚āhaṭ, as in khislāhaṭ, q.v.
def: The state of being put to shame, mortification, humiliation; vexation; — shamefacedness, bashfulness; — peevishness, fretfulness.

ety: khisiyān, for khisiyānā; ˚pat and ˚paṭ, prob. fr. S. vr̤itti; ˚pan and ˚panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚āhaṭ, as in khislāhaṭ, q.v.
def: The state of being put to shame, mortification, humiliation; vexation; — shamefacedness, bashfulness; — peevishness, fretfulness.

v.t. To move, move away, remove; to draw back or away; to shove off or away, to drive away, to put to flight; to put out of place, to displace; — to put off, to postpone; — to push (towards), to slip (into the hands of); — to decamp with, to carry off, to steal
subs: To make off with, to carry off, to steal.

def: s.m. One who slinks away, &c.; — one who has taken leave of his senses.
adj. (f. -ī), Slippery; smooth.
Slipping, sliding; slipperiness.
v.t. To cause to slip, &c.
s.m. Causing to slip; — slipping; slipperiness.

ety: khīs˚ = S. क्लिश्य(ते), pass. of rt. क्लिश
def: v.n. To grin, to gnash or show the teeth; to be mortified.

ety: for A. خسة, inf. n. of خسّ 'to be mean or sordid,' &c.
def: s.f. Meanness, baseness, vileness; sordidness, stinginess, parsimony, avarice, penuriousness.
s.f. = ḵẖishtak, q.v.
s.f. (Dakkhinī), Dirt, filth, impurity, &c. ( = kiṭṭ, q.v.).

ety: inf. n. of iii of خطب 'to recite'
def: s.m. Conversation, discourse, speech, address, harangue; — a title
subs: s.m. Angry or reproachful speech, rebukes, censures.

def: s.f. (Dakkhinī) Giggling, titter; sound of laughing (cf. khilkhil).

ety: for A. خطة ḵẖit̤t̤at, fem.; v.n. fr. خطّ; see ḵẖat̤t̤
def: s.m. A piece of ground (marked out), a plot of land; district, territory, region, country.
adj. (Dakkhinī) Filthy, soiled, polluted, impure (syn. mailā).
s.m. A parterre, a flower-bed.
